title: Centre unlikely to sack Pradhan over NEET row, open to compensate victims' kin: Report
content: The central government will likely not remove the education minister amid NEET paper leak demands.  It believes he is not responsible for the alleged examination irregularities.  The government may consider compensation for students' families who died by suicide.  It is also open to talks with protesters led by the CJP.  Parliament remains the appropriate forum for discussing the issue and finding solutions.

title: 'Protect children's future': Centre seeks dialogue with oppn amid protests - top developments
content: Parliament witnessed disruptions over the alleged NEET paper leak and police action. Opposition MPs marked a 'Black Day' protest demanding the education minister's resignation. The government offered a parliamentary discussion, which the opposition refused without the minister stepping down. Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike, setting conditions for ending it. Courts are examining allegations of police brutality during student demonstrations.

title: 'Sooryavanshi must have rectified where he went wrong': Iyer ahead of Zimbabwe series
content: Captain Shreyas Iyer expressed confidence in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of the Zimbabwe series.  The young batter faced challenges during his recent England tour.  Iyer believes Sooryavanshi has learned from his experiences and is ready.  The team aims to play with freedom and recover from past tours.  Sooryavanshi feels supported and confident returning to Harare.

title: From oil to atoms: Donald Trump to open nuclear door to Saudis
content: The Trump White House is set to announce a significant nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia. This agreement allows for extensive civilian nuclear cooperation and uranium enrichment on Saudi soil. Critics express concerns about nuclear proliferation and regional security implications. The US administration argues this approach counters Russian and Chinese influence in the region. Lawmakers will scrutinize the deal, which may weaken existing non-proliferation safeguards.

title: Ford & Aramark probing Ford factory kiosks after employees lost jobs for 'stealing'
content: Ford and Aramark are reviewing workplace self-service kiosks. This review follows employee dismissals for alleged snack theft incidents. Workers claim payment failures and software glitches caused wrongful accusations. Some recently fired employees have been reinstated after investigations cleared them. The companies are investigating kiosk functionality issues in limited cases.

title: 'For the sake of India's future': Yuvraj calls for 'dialogue' amid NEET protests
content: Yuvraj Singh urged dialogue and peace for students amid NEET exam protests. He stressed that children deserve safe learning environments to chase their dreams. Protests over the alleged NEET paper leak have intensified across the country. Parliament has faced disruptions, and legal scrutiny continues over the issue. Manu Bhaker also voiced concerns for students' futures and fair opportunities.

title: Trump's 200% tariff plan may be a bitter pill for US consumers, warns Dr Reddy's
content: The United States plans significant tariffs on imported generic medicines. These duties will rise to 200% in the coming years. Dr Reddy's Laboratories anticipates higher medicine prices for American consumers. Shifting manufacturing to the US is currently considered impractical by the company. India faces substantial exposure as a major generic medicine exporter to the US.

title: 'He was struggling': Ex-India cricketer slams Shubman Gill's captaincy vs England
content: Former India batter Hanuma Vihari questioned Shubman Gill's leadership after a recent ODI defeat. Vihari highlighted concerns regarding the team's limited bowling options during the crucial match. He also expressed confusion over Kuldeep Yadav's exclusion from the playing eleven. The former player emphasized the need for experienced match-winners in ODIs. Vihari believes Gill needs tactical improvements in his captaincy approach.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: Meghalaya homestay mission: How to get 70% subsidy to start or upgrade a hostel
content: Meghalaya is offering a 70 percent subsidy for homestay development ahead of the National Games 2027. This initiative aims to create entrepreneurship and long-term tourism businesses for local residents. Applicants can receive grants for new constructions and upgrades, with financial and technical support provided. The scheme also includes training programs and digital platform listings for increased visibility.

title: Ford worker says he was fired over $1.95 cookies despite proof of payment
content: A Ford employee faced termination after an alleged cookie theft from a self-checkout kiosk. Bank records later proved the $1.95 snack had been paid for by the diabetic worker. Ford acknowledged the error and offered the employee back wages and his old job. However, the former electrician declined the offer and is now preparing to sue the automaker. This incident highlights concerns about workplace technology and employee investigation protocols.

title: In 1955, Hawaii released cannibal snails. Native tree snails paid the price
content: Hawaii introduced a predatory snail in 1955 to control pests, which then devastated native tree snails. Scientists now operate a snail emergency room to protect the remaining species. These colorful kāhuli snails were once abundant and culturally significant to Hawaiians. Captive breeding is slow, and efforts focus on predator-proof fences for reintroduction. Conservationists strive to prevent further extinctions, highlighting a global trend of human-induced endangerment.

title: Samsung's biggest launches: Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Watch & everything new announced
content: Samsung unveiled three new foldable phones and two smartwatches with advanced capabilities. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Ultra offer enhanced displays and powerful processors. The Galaxy Z Flip 8 introduces an AI-driven cover screen for seamless interaction. New smartwatches provide advanced health tracking and rugged designs for users. These devices are available for pre-order with sales commencing soon.

title: From Miami to Maui: Jeff Bezos' $800 million-plus real estate empire
content: Jeff Bezos has amassed a vast residential portfolio across the United States. His property collection spans from Washington state to South Florida and Hawaii. Bezos acquired multiple neighboring properties to expand his existing compounds. He also owns two luxury yachts, Koru and Abeona, which are assets afloat. This extensive real estate buying pattern reflects his significant wealth and investments.

title: Watch: TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh marshalled out, suspended after assembly ruckus
content: Trinamool Congress MLA Kunal Ghosh faced suspension from the West Bengal Assembly. He disrupted proceedings by interrupting a fellow legislator during a budget debate. Marshals forcibly removed Ghosh after he refused to return to his seat. This incident highlighted internal rifts within the Trinamool Congress legislature party. Assembly proceedings resumed only after the legislator was escorted from the House.

title: Firouzja wins Chennai Grand Masters as Gukesh suffers shock last-place finish
content: French Grandmaster Alireza Firouzja secured the Chennai Grand Masters title. He held Indian GM Arjun Erigaisi to a draw in the final round. World champion D Gukesh endured a disappointing campaign, finishing last in the tournament. Firouzja finished with 4.5 points after two victories and five draws. Andreikin ended the event as the only other unbeaten player.

title: Candace Owens slams Erika Kirk, says Charlie Kirk fueled her fame ambitions
content: Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.

title: Ines Garcia breaks silence after brutal online hate over past relationship
content: Lamine Yamal's girlfriend, Ines Garcia, issued an emotional statement after facing online abuse over unverified claims about a past relationship. Asking for empathy, she reminded users that there is "someone who feels" behind every account. Garcia, who supported Yamal throughout Spain's 2026 FIFA World Cup-winning campaign, also thanked those who stood by her during the backlash.

title: Terrell Owens' $25K donation raises Nolan Wells case reward to $125K
content: Former NFL star Terrell Owens donated $25,000 to the reward fund tied to Nolan Wells' death investigation, raising the total to $125,000. Owens joins Tyler Perry and Rev. Al Sharpton, who each contributed $50,000. Attorney Benjamin Crump urged anyone with information to come forward as investigators continue probing the mysterious death of the 18-year-old college football player after a Fourth of July trip to Mississippi's Horn Island.

title: How the world's happiest nation plans to stop youth exodus
content: Bhutan, known for its unique happiness philosophy, is experiencing a troubling trend of youth emigration. In response, the government is introducing Gelephu Mindfulness City, which aims to seamlessly integrate economic advancement with cultural preservation and ecological sustainability. Supported by India through infrastructure development and rail connectivity, this initiative hopes to inspire young Bhutanese to choose to stay and thrive in their country.

title: 'US will destroy one bridge or power plant': Trump warns Iran over Hormuz attacks
content: United States forces will destroy Iranian infrastructure if Tehran fires at ships. This threat comes after recent American airstrikes and Iranian missile attacks on Jordan. Diplomatic efforts show little progress as officials remain firm on their positions. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz significantly impacts global energy markets and economies. Houthi rebels in Yemen also threaten shipping, putting another trade chokepoint at risk.

title: Jaffer says Rahul 'is at par' with Kohli, reveals one quality that separates them
content: Wasim Jaffer believes KL Rahul possesses talent equal to Virat Kohli. He noted Rahul's technical skill and composure in all formats. Jaffer feels Rahul has underachieved despite his considerable potential. Kohli's elite mentality and consistency create a significant difference between the two players. This mental edge has allowed Kohli to achieve unprecedented success in cricket.

title: Universal provident fund scheme: How PF for gig workers, self-employed could work
content: The proposed framework aims to allow unorganised sector workers and self-employed to set aside a portion of their income for deposits under a universal provident fund scheme which will earn regular interest on par with the existing EPFO scheme.

title: 'Lots of positives': Iyer on 'rough' England tour, urges fearless approach in Zimbabwe
content: India captain Shreyas Iyer views the recent England tour as a foundation for future success.  He believes the challenging phase has brought the squad closer together.  Iyer urged his young team to embrace an attacking mindset and not fear failure.  The team faces Zimbabwe in a three-match T20I series starting Thursday.  He also warned against underestimating the experienced Zimbabwe squad.

title: Politics of silence: Is Sharad Pawar keeping options open on Delimitation Bill?
content: The silence of Sharad Pawar's NCP(SP) regarding the Delimitation Bill is stirring political rumors. While Sharad Pawar joined the opposition for demonstrations on Tuesday, he met Prime Minister Narendra Modi the next day. Supriya Sule, his daughter, expressed conditional support for Bill if it proposed seat increases across states evenly. This reflects Pawar's history of maintaining strategic ambiguity, thereby ensuring his party remains adaptable within shifting political currents.

title: Meet Polly Cooper, who walked over 200 miles in the snow to feed Washington’s starving army
content: Polly Cooper, an Oneida woman, journeyed 200 miles to deliver essential food supplies to the starving Continental Army. She prepared corn and taught soldiers how to cook, saving many lives during a harsh winter. Cooper refused payment for her actions, receiving only a shawl and bonnet as thanks. Her vital contributions were preserved through oral tradition and later documented by historians. The U.S.

title: Delhi ACP injured in fresh stone-pelting near Tolstoy Marg as CJP protest continues at Jantar Mantar
content: In a recent escalation of tensions, an assistant commissioner of police sustained injuries amid renewed stone-pelting during protests by the Cockroach Janta Party at Jantar Mantar. Protesters attempted to march towards Parliament, resulting in clashes with law enforcement. The CJP signaled a willingness to engage in discussions at a neutral site. In response to the unrest, security measures have been intensified throughout New Delhi.

title: 'I would have headbutted him!': Zlatan slams Paredes, asks why Spain players 'just watched'
content: Zlatan Ibrahimovic strongly criticised Argentine midfielder Leandro Paredes for his actions.  He stated he would have headbutted Paredes if he had been on the field.  Ibrahimovic also questioned the Spanish players' lack of response to the confrontation.  FIFA is investigating Paredes' conduct following the match.  Paredes later expressed pride in representing his country despite the loss.

title: NEET impasse deepens: Goverment says 'discuss', opposition says 'Pradhan must go'
content: The government stated readiness for a Parliament discussion on the NEET exam irregularities. Opposition parties insisted on the Union Education Minister's resignation before any debate. Repeated disruptions in Rajya Sabha led to adjournments during the Monsoon Session. Protests continued inside and outside Parliament over the alleged paper leak. The government accused the Opposition of avoiding discussion by setting preconditions.

title: Delhi HC seeks govt's response on pleas over alleged brutality during CJP march
content: The Delhi High Court issued a notice to the Delhi government on Wednesday.  This action follows petitions alleging police brutality against students during a recent march.  The court has directed the government to preserve all relevant digital records.  These records include CCTV footage and body camera recordings from the incident.  The matter has been scheduled for its next hearing on September eleventh.

title: BPCL posts Rs 3,962 crore Q1 loss as high crude prices squeeze fuel margins
content: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd reported a significant net loss for the April-June quarter. Elevated crude oil prices and regulated fuel rates severely impacted marketing margins. The company incurred substantial LPG under-recoveries and unpaid subsidy dues. Revenue from operations increased, but total expenses climbed significantly. Fuel sales and refinery processing saw a slight decline during the period.

title: 'No legal action against CJP protesters': Wangchuk sets condition to end hunger strike
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk will end his hunger strike if protesters face no legal action. He seeks assurance from the government regarding the CJP's march participants. Wangchuk also requested compensation for affected students and parliamentary discussion. He wants to return to his work and students after receiving this assurance. The activist will continue his fast indefinitely without this government guarantee.

title: 'No one has ever done this before': Harbhajan makes massive Sooryavanshi claim
content: Harbhajan Singh lauded Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's exceptional talent and fearless batting approach. He noted the youngster's confidence mirrors modern cricketers' evolving mindset. Harbhajan recalled Rahul Dravid's question about Sooryavanshi's first IPL ball. The former spinner shared Sachin Tendulkar's advice on maintaining focus and dedication. This guidance shapes Harbhajan's own counsel to aspiring young cricketers.

title: 'We literally have the paper': US says India trade deal could be signed in months
content: A significant India-US trade agreement is expected within three to four months. Negotiations are nearly finalized, with the text effectively complete and ready for signing. The primary remaining hurdle involves ongoing US trade investigations, not bilateral disagreements. Washington's Section 301 probes are nearing conclusion, which will allow progress. This agreement aims to expand market access and deepen economic ties between both nations.

title: Trump wants FIFA chief Infantino as next UN secretary-general: Report
content: US President Donald Trump reportedly supports FIFA president Gianni Infantino for the UN secretary-general position. Trump believes Infantino possesses the ability to unite people globally and is a strong candidate. The two developed a closer relationship during the recent FIFA World Cup tournament. Infantino had previously presented Trump with FIFA's inaugural Peace Prize award. A candidate needs UN Security Council backing and General Assembly approval for the role.

title: Running on rollovers: Pakistan's reserves get $27 billion foreign loans from Saudi, China
content: Pakistan secured approximately twenty-seven billion dollars in foreign funding last fiscal year. Saudi Arabia and China provided significant rollover support for existing loans. Fresh borrowing accounted for sixteen billion dollars of the total inflows. This foreign assistance primarily financed the budget and repaid external debt. Pakistan's exports declined by six percent during the same period.

title: Waited over 5 years for electricity connection, consumer wins Rs 40,000 compensation
content: The Delhi consumer commission found BSES Rajdhani Power guilty of service deficiency.  The power company took over five years to provide an electricity connection.  BSES could not prove it informed the consumer about application discrepancies.  The commission upheld compensation and litigation costs awarded by the district forum.  BSES must pay the awarded amount within forty-five days.

title: Jeetendra's Rs 4.25 lakh pali hill deal is now worth Rs 450 crore
content: Jeetendra purchased Bharat Bhushan's bungalow for a modest sum.  He later sold the Burma teak from the demolished structure for profit.  This initial investment laid the groundwork for his successful real estate career.  The property's value has since appreciated significantly over the years.  Today, the land alone is worth an estimated Rs 450 crore.

title: Why did stock market crash today? Sensex ends over 700 points down, Nifty below 24,000
content: Pharmaceutical stocks were among the biggest drags on the market after Trump announced a phased tariff framework for imported generic medicines, giving drugmakers a two-year transition period before higher duties come into force.

title: India commissions INS Malvan to beef up sub-hunter fleet to counter China threat
content: The Indian Navy commissioned Malvan, its second Mahe-class vessel, at Karwar naval base. This new warship will bolster the nation's submarine hunter fleet in the Indian Ocean. The vessel is designed for shallow water anti-submarine operations and mine warfare. India is inducting sixteen specialized vessels to secure its extensive coastline. This move strengthens maritime security amid growing regional naval competition.

title: Kuldeep Yadav lands county deal after no-show in India vs England ODIs
content: Kuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire as an overseas player for the remainder of the English domestic season. The India spinner will play five One-Day Cup matches and three County Championship games. The move comes after he was left out of India's ODI series in England. Yorkshire believe Kuldeep's experience and left-arm wrist spin will strengthen their bowling attack.

title: Threat to CM Vijay: Vilathikulam DMK MLA’s bail plea dismissed
content: The prosecution said the bail petition should be rejected as the accused had issued serious threat to the Chief Minister

title: What caused the NHPC tunnel disaster in Sikkim’s Namchi? | Explained
content: Methane trapped inside rock is suspected to have triggered the fatal blast. But how does this happen?

title: Student bodies to hold ‘Chalo Lok Bhavan’ protest on July 23
content: They seek Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and call for support to the July 24 educational institutions bandh in Andhra Pradesh

title: Online taxi fares surge as drivers continue ‘offline strike’ in Kerala
content: Commuters face higher fares and cab shortages, forcing many to switch to buses and autorickshaws; drivers demand fare revisions, welfare measures and action against illegal bike taxis; talks with Uber scheduled on Thursday

title: INDIA Bloc MPs stopped from meeting Wangchuk
content: Sonam Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali J. Angmo said her husband’s “vital parameters are fine, but muscle mass is decreasing”. “It is not life threatening, but he has weakened,” she said

title: PM silent in Parliament, forced to protest outside his residence: Akhilesh
content: Akhilesh Yadav said the Opposition, students and youth were unanimous in seeking Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation before any discussion in Parliament

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Internet shut down, police use tear gas at Sansad Marg, as crowd of protesters swells
content: Rahul Gandhi using students' issues for political mileage, says J.P. Nadda; Wangchuk seeks 'unequivocal assurance' that no young protester will face punitive, retaliatory legal action

title: Over 47 lakh Karnataka voters flagged for possible deletion during electoral roll revision so far
content: The cases will undergo further verification by Booth Level Agents appointed by recognised political parties, and each case will be scrutinised by Electoral Registration Officers before any decision on deletion is taken

title: Supreme Court asks States, UTs to consider raising retirement age of judicial officers to 61
content: The court, however, clarified that the interim arrangement would not prejudice its adjudication of the larger question of whether the retirement age of district judicial officers should be uniformly enhanced from 60 to 62 years across the country.

title: ABVP seeks panel for reforms in competitive examinations
content: Student body urges greater transparency in NEET-UG and other entrance tests, calls for institutional reforms and reiterates demand for a CBI inquiry into alleged examination irregularities

title: Mamata loyalist Kunal Ghosh marshalled out of West Bengal Assembly after he disrupts dissident MLA’s speech
content: Dispute between two rival groups of Trinamool Congress has been brewing over the past two days over who will  be given priority in speaking in the House during discussions

title: Pidari Ekaveeri: a fierce Goddess, a captivating smile, and Raja Raja’s mother-in-law’s grant
content: R. Kalaikovan of the Dr. Rajamanickanar Centre for Historical Research says the idols is the probably oldest identifiable Pidari image in Tamil Nadu. Although several inscriptions from the Chola period refer to Pidari deities, the corresponding images have rarely been identified, he adds

title: Rights panel orders timely release of subsistence allowance
content: The order was issued while considering a complaint that the subsistence allowance due to a suspended traditional chenda artist at the Maha Shiva Temple, Thriprangode, had not been released as required

title: Tamil Nadu makes a mark at Bharat Tex 2026
content: Tiruppur Exporters’ Association optimistic about getting orders from overseas buyers who visited the fair

title: Kochi metro Phase II: KMRL awards ₹91.6-crore contract to Siemens
content: Contract covers communication systems, including passenger information displays, CCTV, fibre-optic connectivity and emergency communication facilities

title: Andhra Pradesh registers six new COVID cases, three each from East Godavari, Vizag districts
content: The total case load in the State rises to 32, while the death toll stands at four, says Health Department

title: Waqf Board chairperson slams IUML, Kerala govt. for favouring non-inclusion of Muslims on State Board
content: K.S. Hamsa accuses the State government of siding with Sangh Parivar affiliates, who, according to him, are out to destabilise the board

title: Centre positive on extending relief to tobacco, aqua farmers, says Agriculture Minister Atchannaidu
content: The Minister accuses YSR Congress Party president Jagan Mohan Reddy of exploiting the problems of tobacco and aquaculture farmers for political mileage

title: Kerala CM Satheesan making false claim on SC rejecting petitions against Waqf amendment Bill: Rajeeve
content: He says  Chief Minister is speaking like a spokesperson of Bharatiya Janata Party, by echoing its stance on including non-Muslims in an institution related to Muslims

title: Centre weighs easing cross-ownership rules between airlines and airports
content: Current rules cap airline ownership in airports and are inferred to similarly restrict airport operators from owning airlines

title: Delhi HC seeks Centre’s reply to claims of police brutality, excess force during Parliament march
content: High Court orders preservation of CCTV, video footage; says allegations cannot be dismissed as “isolated” incidents; ASG claims protesters turned violent; petitioners allege police used lathis with nails, electric batons, molested women

title: 50 OTT platforms disabled for displaying obscene content, other violations: Govt.
content: IT Rules require social media platforms to remove unlawful content within three hours of the receipt of a court order or reasoned intimation by the government or its agency

title: Police crack down on AISA march in Patna seeking resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Hundreds of AISA activists, joined by local students and members of the Left party’s student wing, first gathered at Gandhi Maidan and tried to march towards Lok Bhavan

title: Scouts and guides course mandatory in VTU-affiliated institutions
content: According to the Union Education Ministry and the Higher Education Department of Karnataka, ‘Rangers and Rovers’ should be compulsory in all higher educational institutions, but this has not been implemented

title: India joins Quad partners in call for Indo-Pacific peace, ASEAN centrality amid Chinese assertiveness
content: A joint statement said the Quad reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening a free and open Indo-Pacific while extending “unwavering support” for “ASEAN unity and centrality”

title: Police use water cannon as Youth Congress, KSU march against police action on Congress leaders in Delhi turns tense
content: Protest  condemns the alleged manhandling of Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi by the Delhi Police during a demonstration demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged NEET irregularities

title: A.P. simplifies removal of lands from 22A prohibited list, strengthens property rights
content: Authorities have made it mandatory to issue show-cause notices before including any land in prohibited list, provide 30 days for objections and 14 more days for legal arguments

title: Woman’s hand severed by partner in Kadakkavoor
content: Domestic disputes allegedly triggered the brutal assault, resulting in head injuries and a severed hand.

title: Will end fast if Centre assures no action against protesters: Wangchuk writes to Nadda, Singh
content: He also said that Union Ministers J.P. Nadda and Jitendra Singh had already assured him that the government would positively consider his two other key demands

title: APSRTC employees’ JAC seeks settlement of long-pending demands
content: JAC calls for PRC implementation, clearance of pending dues, recruitment, procurement of 4,000 buses and withdrawal of privatisation measures

title: Greens decry delay in remediation of Kuzhikandam creek in Kochi as PCB redraws plan
content: High Court directs PCB to revise implementation plan and complete key remedial works by year-end; greens say prolonged delay has allowed pollution and civic problems to persist; funding gap and recovery of remediation costs from polluting industries yet to be resolved

title: Andhra Pradesh govt. invites tenders for implementation of Sanjeevani scheme
content: The pre-bid meeting is scheduled for July 27 and the last date for submission of tenders is August 14

title: Sonam Wangchuk Says Will End Fast If Centre Spares Students From Legal Action
content: In the absence of such an assurance, Wangchuk stressed he would be forced to continue his fast indefinitely.

title: German Approval Sparks Security Concerns Over French Russian Nuclear Project
content: The planned joint venture by a subsidiary of French company Framatome and Russia's state-owned nuclear power agency Rosatom is aimed at producing fuel rods used in Russian-designed reactors.

title: Nearly 700 Detained As J&K Police Launch Security Sweep
content: Acting on specific intelligence inputs, police teams fanned out across multiple locations to target the hidden network that keeps terrorism alive on the ground.

title: When Dharmendra Pradhan Hit The Streets To Protest A Paper Leak 30 Years Ago
content: Dharmendra Pradhan hit the streets of Bhubaneswar as a student leader associated with the Akhil Bharatiya Vidyarthi Parishad (ABVP) to protest against the Janaki Patnaik's government.

title: Vijay's 2-Pronged Stance On Protest: Support For Rahul Gandhi, Abolish NEET
content: The TVK founder described police action against the Congress leader as "condemnable". In the same breath, he reiterated his party's oft-repeated demand -- the scrapping of NEET exams.

title: "NEET Paper Leaked Thrice In 5 Years, Yet No Accountability": K Annamalai
content: K Annamalai, a former IPS officer, was one of the BJP's most prominent faces in Tamil Nadu before he quit ahead of the state elections this year.

title: US Will Bomb Bridges, Power Plants If Iran Shoots At Ships In Hormuz: Trump
content: Trump said that it does not matter if the attack is by a missile, rocket or drone; the United States will "bomb and destroy" a bridge or power plant near Iran's capital -- Tehran.

title: Woman Strangled, Body Stuffed Into Suitcase, Dumped In Bengaluru Lake
content: The victim, Jayamma (61), wife of Boregowda and a resident of Challaghatta in Kengeri, had been reported missing.

title: Analysis: Why Congress's Charanjit Channi Dilemma Could Be BJP's Gain
content: Channi also attempted another move to oppose the decision; however, the high command is firm on continuing with Amrinder Singh Raja Warring.

title: Queues, Confusion As Delhi Metro Shuts 16 Stations Over Security Concerns
content: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) has shut 16 stations across several lines including blue, yellow, and violet owing to "security reasons."

title: "Situation Extremely Serious": Himanta Sarma Visits Assam Flood-Hit Districts
content: From April 29 to July 21, floods have affected 25 districts, 74 revenue circles and nearly 1,700 villages, impacting 7,27,506 people across Assam.

title: As Bihar Government Nears 100 Days, A Look At Its Flagship Initiative
content: At the core of the initiative is a system that allows people to take unresolved complaints directly to the highest level of government.

title: Bulgaria Approves US Use Of Bezmer Air Base For Middle East Support
content: Up to eight US tanker aircraft and 250 personnel will be stationed at Bezmer Air Base from Friday to October without further approvals.

title: 'Need Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation First': Opposition On Centre's NEET Discussion Stand
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju underscored that the government had been maintaining since the beginning of the Monsoon Session that it was ready to discuss all important issues, including the NEET exam paper leak.

title: US-India Trade Deal Could Be Signed In 3-4 Months, US Official Says
content: Washington and New Delhi have been negotiating a bilateral trade agreement to expand market access and lower trade barriers as part of efforts to deepen economic ties.

title: Adani Power Q1 Profit Jumps 47% To Rs 4,867 Crore
content: Adani Power said that consolidated power sale volume was higher by 16.9 per cent at 28.8 billion unit (BU) in Q1 FY27 compared to 24.6 BU in Q1 FY26, on the back of higher operating capacity and strong power demand growth.

title: Adani Green Energy Q1 Capacity Rises 27%, EBITDA At Record High
content: The company's operational renewable energy capacity increased to 20,142 MW from 15,815 MW a year earlier, reflecting a 27 per cent year-on-year growth, according to its stock exchange filing.

title: US Plans To Announce Nuclear Agreement With Saudi Arabia Amid Iran Tensions
content: The move comes as Washington fights a war with Iran that began in part over US concerns with Tehran's own nuclear program -- an issue that remains a key point of contention during now-stalled talks between the two foes.

title: 'Will Return To India By Early Next Year': Lalit Modi After Tribunal Verdict
content: Referring to the ruling, Lalit Modi, who lives abroad, said his 16-year-long legal fight has come to fruition.
